title: Do not hardcode secrets or long-lived credentials
text: Hardcoded tokens, passwords, API keys, and similar credentials create rotation, exposure, and audit problems. Prefer runtime-provided secrets with narrowly scoped access.
summary: Secret-like literals should live in secret management or deployment configuration, not in source.
embedding_text:
rule_no: SEC.1
pack_id: shared_security
language_id: shared
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: SEC
title: Do not hardcode secrets or long-lived credentials
summary: Secret-like literals should live in secret management or deployment configuration, not in source.
keywords: not, secrets, credentials, sec.1, hardcode, long-lived, secret-like, literals, should, live, secret, management
category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: hardcoded_secret
fix_guidance: Move the secret into environment-backed configuration or a secret manager and inject it at runtime.
text: Hardcoded tokens, passwords, API keys, and similar credentials create rotation, exposure, and audit problems. Prefer runtime-provided secrets with narrowly scoped access.
fix_guidance: Move the secret into environment-backed configuration or a secret manager and inject it at runtime.

title: Build shell commands from trusted primitives, not concatenated strings
text: Command construction through string concatenation is fragile and exposes injection risk. Prefer argument vectors, explicit allow-lists, or dedicated APIs.
summary: Shell execution paths should not interpolate untrusted fragments into a command string.
embedding_text:
rule_no: SEC.2
pack_id: shared_security
language_id: shared
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: SEC
title: Build shell commands from trusted primitives, not concatenated strings
summary: Shell execution paths should not interpolate untrusted fragments into a command string.
keywords: shell, not, command, sec.2, build, commands, trusted, primitives, concatenated, strings, execution, paths
category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: shell_injection
fix_guidance: Use structured subprocess or exec APIs that separate the executable from validated arguments.
text: Command construction through string concatenation is fragile and exposes injection risk. Prefer argument vectors, explicit allow-lists, or dedicated APIs.
fix_guidance: Use structured subprocess or exec APIs that separate the executable from validated arguments.

title: Parameterize SQL instead of composing query text dynamically
text: SQL should be parameterized through the database driver's bind facilities or a higher-level abstraction. Dynamic text assembly should be reserved for carefully controlled structural clauses.
summary: Query text built from variables is difficult to reason about and easy to misuse.
embedding_text:
rule_no: SEC.3
pack_id: shared_security
language_id: shared
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: SEC
title: Parameterize SQL instead of composing query text dynamically
summary: Query text built from variables is difficult to reason about and easy to misuse.
keywords: text, sql, query, should, sec.3, parameterize, instead, composing, dynamically, built, variables, difficult
category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: sql_injection, dynamic_sql
fix_guidance: Bind values separately from the SQL string and constrain structural query changes through allow-listed fragments.
text: SQL should be parameterized through the database driver's bind facilities or a higher-level abstraction. Dynamic text assembly should be reserved for carefully controlled structural clauses.
fix_guidance: Bind values separately from the SQL string and constrain structural query changes through allow-listed fragments.

title: Do not leave fallback secrets or test credentials in production code paths
text: Even when intended only for local development or bootstrap, embedded fallback secrets create a second and weaker trust path that is easy to forget during incident response or key rotation.
summary: Backup credentials often become the least-audited credential in the system.
embedding_text:

language_id: shared
context_id: 
dialect_id:

keywords: fallback, secrets, credentials, sec.4, not, leave, test, production, code, paths, backup, often
category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: hardcoded_secret
fix_guidance: Remove embedded fallback credentials and require runtime configuration or a dedicated local-only development path instead.
text: Even when intended only for local development or bootstrap, embedded fallback secrets create a second and weaker trust path that is easy to forget during incident response or key rotation.
fix_guidance: Remove embedded fallback credentials and require runtime configuration or a dedicated local-only development path instead.

title: Keep command execution APIs structured so the executable and arguments remain distinct
text: Separating the executable from validated arguments narrows the injection surface and makes the trust boundary visible in code review across languages and runtimes.
summary: Security review is much stronger when command construction never depends on ad hoc shell parsing.
embedding_text:

context_id:

title: Keep command execution APIs structured so the executable and arguments remain distinct

category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: shell_injection
fix_guidance: Use structured process APIs, validate each argument according to its contract, and avoid passing shell fragments through string concatenation.
text: Separating the executable from validated arguments narrows the injection surface and makes the trust boundary visible in code review across languages and runtimes.
fix_guidance: Use structured process APIs, validate each argument according to its contract, and avoid passing shell fragments through string concatenation.

title: Dynamic query structure should stay allow-listed even when values are parameterized
text: ORDER BY fragments, table identifiers, and optional clauses can still create injection or correctness risk when the allowed structures are not tightly bounded.
summary: Parameter binding protects values, not uncontrolled structural SQL changes.

category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: dynamic_sql
fix_guidance: Keep structural variation behind a small allow-list and continue binding values separately from the SQL text.
text: ORDER BY fragments, table identifiers, and optional clauses can still create injection or correctness risk when the allowed structures are not tightly bounded.
fix_guidance: Keep structural variation behind a small allow-list and continue binding values separately from the SQL text.

title: Example, bootstrap, and fallback credentials in committed code should be treated as real secrets unless clearly impossible to activate
text: Backup and bootstrap secrets often become the least-audited authentication path in a system because everyone assumes they are temporary or unreachable.
summary: "Test" or "dev" labels do not remove rotation, misuse, or confusion risk once credentials are committed.
embedding_text:

section: SEC
title: Example, bootstrap, and fallback credentials in committed code should be treated as real secrets unless clearly impossible to activate
summary: "Test" or "dev" labels do not remove rotation, misuse, or confusion risk once credentials are committed.
keywords: bootstrap, credentials, secrets, sec.7, example, fallback, committed, code, should, treated, real, unless
category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: hardcoded_secret
fix_guidance: Remove committed fallback credentials or move them into an isolated local-only development path that cannot ship as a production default.
text: Backup and bootstrap secrets often become the least-audited authentication path in a system because everyone assumes they are temporary or unreachable.
fix_guidance: Remove committed fallback credentials or move them into an isolated local-only development path that cannot ship as a production default.

title: Quote variable expansions unless deliberate word splitting is the point
text: In shell, expansion rules are powerful but easy to trigger accidentally. Most script variables should remain quoted so inputs are handled as one logical value.
summary: Unquoted expansions are a frequent source of path, glob, and whitespace bugs.
embedding_text:
rule_no: BASH.1
pack_id: project_bash
language_id: bash
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: BASH
title: Quote variable expansions unless deliberate word splitting is the point
summary: Unquoted expansions are a frequent source of path, glob, and whitespace bugs.
keywords: expansions, bash.1, quote, variable, unless, deliberate, word, splitting, point, unquoted, frequent, source
category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: unquoted_variable
fix_guidance: Wrap expansions in double quotes or replace the shell string path with an explicit array form when multiple arguments are intended.
text: In shell, expansion rules are powerful but easy to trigger accidentally. Most script variables should remain quoted so inputs are handled as one logical value.
fix_guidance: Wrap expansions in double quotes or replace the shell string path with an explicit array form when multiple arguments are intended.

title: Keep destructive filesystem commands behind validated paths
text: Scripts that mutate or delete filesystem state should validate the target path and make empty or malformed inputs impossible to interpret as a broader target.
summary: Recursive delete and broad globs deserve explicit guarding.

category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: rm_rf_glob
fix_guidance: Validate the target, quote it, and guard against empty or root-like values before destructive operations.
text: Scripts that mutate or delete filesystem state should validate the target path and make empty or malformed inputs impossible to interpret as a broader target.
fix_guidance: Validate the target, quote it, and guard against empty or root-like values before destructive operations.

title: Build command arguments so expansions stay one logical value by default
text: Most bash values represent one path, token, or user input. Leaving the expansion unquoted delegates too much behavior to shell word splitting and globbing rules.
summary: Shell variables should not silently turn into multiple arguments unless the script makes that intent explicit.

category: script_safety
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: unquoted_variable
fix_guidance: Quote expansions by default or switch to arrays when the script truly intends to pass multiple arguments.
text: Most bash values represent one path, token, or user input. Leaving the expansion unquoted delegates too much behavior to shell word splitting and globbing rules.
fix_guidance: Quote expansions by default or switch to arrays when the script truly intends to pass multiple arguments.

title: Destructive path operations should reject empty and root-like values before expansion
text: A defensive shell script treats rm, mv, and similar operations as trust boundaries. Empty variables, unexpected prefixes, or accidental globs should fail closed instead of broadening the target set.
summary: Filesystem mutation is safer when the guardrail exists before the shell interprets the path.

category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: rm_rf_glob, unquoted_variable
fix_guidance: Guard for empty strings, slash-like roots, and unsafe prefixes before running the destructive command.
text: A defensive shell script treats rm, mv, and similar operations as trust boundaries. Empty variables, unexpected prefixes, or accidental globs should fail closed instead of broadening the target set.
fix_guidance: Guard for empty strings, slash-like roots, and unsafe prefixes before running the destructive command.

title: Checked-in automation should not casually depend on sudo-driven privilege escalation
text: A script that requires sudo should make that boundary explicit and narrow. Embedding sudo throughout routine control flow makes review, testing, and non-interactive execution harder to reason about.
summary: Privilege escalation inside normal script flow hides environment assumptions and expands failure modes.

title: Checked-in automation should not casually depend on sudo-driven privilege escalation
summary: Privilege escalation inside normal script flow hides environment assumptions and expands failure modes.
keywords: should, privilege, escalation, script, flow, sudo, bash.5, checked-in, automation, not, casually, depend
category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: sudo_usage
fix_guidance: Isolate privileged steps behind a dedicated boundary or document the explicit operator/runbook contract instead of scattering sudo through normal script logic.
text: A script that requires sudo should make that boundary explicit and narrow. Embedding sudo throughout routine control flow makes review, testing, and non-interactive execution harder to reason about.
fix_guidance: Isolate privileged steps behind a dedicated boundary or document the explicit operator/runbook contract instead of scattering sudo through normal script logic.

title: Prefer strict shell options for scripts that automate state changes
text: Strict shell options reduce a class of accidental success states, especially in CI or deployment-oriented scripts that make stateful changes.
summary: Scripts are easier to reason about when unset variables and failed pipelines do not pass silently.

summary: Scripts are easier to reason about when unset variables and failed pipelines do not pass silently.
keywords: scripts, strict, shell, options, bash.safe.1, prefer, automate, state, changes, easier, reason, about
category: error_handling
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: set_e_missing
fix_guidance: Enable the relevant strict options early or document the narrower failure semantics intentionally chosen for the script.
text: Strict shell options reduce a class of accidental success states, especially in CI or deployment-oriented scripts that make stateful changes.
fix_guidance: Enable the relevant strict options early or document the narrower failure semantics intentionally chosen for the script.

title: Do not pipe remote downloads directly into a shell interpreter
text: Even trusted installation flows are safer when content is fetched, validated, and executed as a separate step instead of a single opaque pipeline.
summary: Streaming remote content into execution removes the normal review and validation boundary.

category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: curl_pipe_shell
fix_guidance: Download to a file, verify provenance or checksum, and execute in a separate explicitly reviewed step.
text: Even trusted installation flows are safer when content is fetched, validated, and executed as a separate step instead of a single opaque pipeline.
fix_guidance: Download to a file, verify provenance or checksum, and execute in a separate explicitly reviewed step.

title: Pipelines that change state should usually enable pipefail with fail-fast options
text: Deployment and automation scripts should make pipeline failure semantics explicit. Without pipefail and related strict options, a later successful command can mask the upstream failure that mattered.
summary: Partial pipeline success can hide the step that actually failed.
embedding_text:

category: error_handling
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: set_e_missing
fix_guidance: Enable pipefail and the strict options the script can support, or document why a narrower failure model is intentional.
text: Deployment and automation scripts should make pipeline failure semantics explicit. Without pipefail and related strict options, a later successful command can mask the upstream failure that mattered.
fix_guidance: Enable pipefail and the strict options the script can support, or document why a narrower failure model is intentional.

title: Remote installer flows should separate fetch, verify, and execute stages
text: An installation script is easier to review and safer to audit when the fetched artifact is visible, verifiable, and executed only after that verification step succeeds.
summary: Combining download and execution into one pipeline removes the point where provenance can be checked.

context_id:

summary: Combining download and execution into one pipeline removes the point where provenance can be checked.
keywords: bash.safe.4, remote, installer, flows, should, separate, fetch, verify, execute, stages, combining, download
category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: curl_pipe_shell
fix_guidance: Fetch to a temporary file, verify the source or checksum, and execute the reviewed artifact in a separate command.
text: An installation script is easier to review and safer to audit when the fetched artifact is visible, verifiable, and executed only after that verification step succeeds.
fix_guidance: Fetch to a temporary file, verify the source or checksum, and execute the reviewed artifact in a separate command.

title: TLS verification bypass in curl should be exceptional and paired with an explicit trust story
text: A script that disables TLS verification during artifact fetch should make the replacement trust mechanism explicit, because the default secure transport boundary has been removed.
summary: `--insecure` or `-k` removes one of the few transport guarantees the script can easily rely on.

category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: curl_insecure
fix_guidance: Keep certificate verification enabled, or replace it with an explicit pinned trust mechanism such as a vetted CA, checksum, or signed artifact workflow.
text: A script that disables TLS verification during artifact fetch should make the replacement trust mechanism explicit, because the default secure transport boundary has been removed.
fix_guidance: Keep certificate verification enabled, or replace it with an explicit pinned trust mechanism such as a vetted CA, checksum, or signed artifact workflow.

title: Privileged shell steps should be isolated from low-trust input shaping
text: When a script mixes user-controlled values, path expansion, and sudo in the same flow, the review burden grows quickly and mistakes have a larger blast radius.
summary: Shell privilege changes deserve a smaller and more reviewable execution boundary than ordinary script logic.
